instruction: Apply one final cleanup pass to Phase 1 before starting Phase 2. Do not implement directory features yet.

workspace.status: Phase 1 compile blockers appear resolved, but there are still two important foundation issues.

review_findings.issue: API base URL is hardcoded to http://localhost:8000, which is wrong for Android emulator/device development.
details: On Android emulator, localhost points to the emulator itself, not the host machine running the FastAPI backend.
For emulator-based local development, host access typically needs 10.0.2.2, or the app needs an environment/config-driven base URL.
The API base URL should be centralized and configurable, not hardcoded to localhost.

review_findings.issue: Router allows protected routes to render while auth restoration is still in the initial state.
details: Returning null during isInitial means the app can briefly render the shell route at / before auth restoration finishes.
Foundation should have a splash/loading gate for auth bootstrap so protected screens do not flash before redirect.

required_fixes.task: Make API base URL configurable for mobile development.
requirements: Move the base URL into a dedicated config layer or app constants module.
Support a sane Android-local-development default such as 10.0.2.2 when appropriate, or clearly isolate the URL so it can be changed in one place.
Do not leave localhost hardcoded inside ApiClient.
When auth state is initial, show a lightweight splash/loading screen instead of allowing protected routes to render.
Only enter shell routes after auth restoration finishes.
Keep the existing login redirect behavior after restoration completes.

acceptance_criteria: ApiClient no longer hardcodes localhost inside the network layer.
The project has a centralized API base URL configuration path.
App startup shows a loading/bootstrap screen while auth restoration is running.
Protected shell screens do not render before auth initialization completes.
No Phase 2 directory/business logic is added.

after_completion: Explain how base URL configuration now works for Android development.
Explain how auth bootstrap routing now works.
